content.question: What is the precise causal magnitude of the brain drain externality (peer composition and teacher incentive effects) on non-selective primary schools from loss of top-performing students at ages 10 and 12?
content.reduction_path: Conduct matched difference-in-differences analysis comparing schools with varying proportions of students entering early-selective tracks, using administrative data on school composition changes, teacher retention, and value-added measures before and after selective enrollment waves.
